utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hello , mrs . peterson .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: hi , doctor taylor . good to see you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: you're here for your hip today , or your- your leg today ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes . i hurt my- the- my- top part of my right leg here .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon . i'm seeing mrs . peterson , here , she's a 43-year-old patient . she's here for left leg pain . right leg pain , right leg pain ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: um so , what happened to you ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i was bowling and as i was running up to the lane , i had my bowling ball all the way back , and when i slung it forward , i hit it right into my leg instead of the lane and so then i fell but- yeah-

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: did you get a strike ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no . in fact , i actually dropped the ball and it jumped two lanes over and landed in the other people's gutter .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: terrific , terrific . so , did it swell up on you ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: it- not- did n't seem like it swelled that much .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: what about bruising ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: um , a little bit on the back- back end , that side .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: have- have you been able to walk on it ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: just a little bit . very carefully .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: sore to walk on ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes . it's very sore .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: um , and going upstairs or downstairs , does that bother you at all ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , well , i do n't have stairs , but um , i would avoid that at all costs .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . um , it looks like you had a history of atopic eczema in your past ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes . yes , i have eczema .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and you take uh- uh , fluocinonide for that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes , when it gets really itchy , i'll- i'll use that and it usually takes care of it .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and , it looks like you have a pre- previous surgical history of a colectomy ? what happened there ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes , i had a- um , some diverticulosis and then um , i actually went into diverticulitis and they ended up going in and having to remove a little bit of my colon .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , let me examine you . does it hurt when i push on your leg like that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes , it does .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . if i lift your leg up like this , does that hurt ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , on my exam , you have some significant tenderness to the lateral aspect of your um right upper leg . you do n't seem to have any pain or tenderness with flexion or extension of your um your lower leg . um , are you taking anything for it right now ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i've been going back and forth between taking ibuprofen and tylenol .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . well , my impression is that you- you probably have a contusion , but let's take a look at your x-ray first . hey , dragon . show me the x-ray . yeah , so if you look at this , this is a normal femur . um , really do n't see any evidence of a fracture or any swelling , so it's essentially , a normal x-ray . so , what we're going to do is , i'm going to start you on um an anti-inflammatory . it's going to be mobic 15 milligrams uh , once a day . i want you to use some ice for the pain , um , and it should , honestly , just being a contusion , get better in the next week or so . if it's not getting better , of course , come on back and- and see me .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , sounds good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon . go ahead and um , pres- do the orders and um , procedures uh , as described . come with me , and uh , i'll get you checked out . dragon , go ahead and finish off the note .

detail_list.summary: Right leg pain.
The patient is a 43-year-old female presenting with right leg pain.
The doctor asks the patient to describe the mechanism of injury.
The patient hit her leg with a bowling ball and fell.
The doctor asks if the leg has swollen.
The patient denies significant swelling.
The doctor asks about bruising.
The patient reports mild bruising.
The doctor asks if the patient is able to walk.
The patient reports walking very carefully.
Atopic eczema.
The doctor asks if the patient uses fluocinonide for eczema.
The patient confirms use of fluocinonide topical cream for eczema.
Colectomy history.
The patient had a colectomy following diverticulitis.
The doctor performs a physical exam of the leg.
The patient reports tenderness upon palpation of the leg.
The doctor notes tenderness to the lateral aspect of the right upper leg and no pain with flexion or extension.
The doctor asks what medications the patient is currently taking.
The patient is alternating Tylenol and ibuprofen.
The X-ray is normal with no fractures or dislocations.
The doctor prescribes Mobic 15 mg once a day.
The patient is instructed to use ice for pain and follow up as needed.

case_description: right leg pain; atopic eczema; hx colectomy following diverticulosis
